meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Vocabulary Set 1
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' — all 16 words (thumbs up, art, music, learn, math, problems, important, think, creative, imagination, figure out, amazing, concentrate, remember, teamwork, fun).

questions.question: What does 'important' mean?
options: Something that does not matter at all
Something that matters a lot and should not be ignored
Something that is easy to do
questions.explanation: 'Important' means something that matters a lot. The text says 'Art is important' — it matters a great deal for students.

questions.question: What does 'creative' mean?
options: Unable to think of any ideas
Able to think of new and original ideas
questions.explanation: 'Creative' means able to think of new and original ideas. Art class helps students be creative — they use their own unique ideas to make things.

questions.question: 'Imagination' is the ability to think of things that are not real, or to think creatively.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Imagination' is the ability to picture things in your mind that may not be real. Art helps you use your imagination to create original things.

questions.question: In music class, you have to _______ — focus all your attention on the notes.
questions.hint: To focus closely; to pay full attention
questions.explanation: 'Concentrate' means to focus all your attention on something. Music requires concentration — you must pay careful attention to notes, rhythm, and timing.

questions.question: What does 'teamwork' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Teamwork' means working together as a group. Music teaches teamwork — students play together and must listen to each other.

questions.question: Art makes you '_______.' Choose the best meaning of this word in the sentence 'Art is important because it makes you think.'
options: To use your brain to form ideas
questions.explanation: 'Think' means to use your brain to form ideas and solve problems. Art makes you think — you must use your brain to create and make decisions.

questions.question: In music class, you have to _______ a lot — like all the notes, words, and rhythms.
questions.explanation: 'Remember' means to keep something in your mind and be able to recall it. Music requires you to remember notes, melodies, lyrics, and rhythms.

questions.question: 'Figure out' means to solve a problem or find a way to do something.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Figure out' means to find a solution. The text says 'You figure out how to make amazing things' — you solve creative problems in art.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they NOUNS (things) or ADJECTIVES (describing words)?
questions.explanation: 'Imagination,' 'teamwork,' 'fun,' and 'art' are things (nouns). 'Creative' and 'amazing' are describing words (adjectives) — they describe how something is.

questions.question: The painting looks _______! It is so impressive and beautiful.
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' means very impressive and wonderful. Art class helps students make 'amazing things' — things that impress and delight people.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'learn' correctly?
options: She learns to play the piano — she is getting better every week.
We learn the art very fast yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'Learn' means to gain new knowledge or skills. 'She learns to play the piano' — she is gaining the skill of piano playing over time.

questions.question: What does 'thumbs up' mean as a phrase?
questions.explanation: 'Thumbs up' is an expression meaning approval — 'I agree!' or 'I support this!' The title 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' means the author strongly approves of art and music in school.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Music makes you concentrate and remember.' This summarizes why music is important — it builds concentration and memory skills.

questions.question: 'Problems' in the phrase 'math problems' means something is broken or going wrong.
questions.explanation: In this context, 'problems' means math questions or exercises, like '3 + 4 = ?' It does not mean something is broken. The word 'problem' has different meanings depending on the context.

questions.question: Sort these skills — do you learn them from ART or from MUSIC (according to the text)?
questions.explanation: Art teaches creativity, imagination, and making amazing things. Music teaches concentration, teamwork, and memory. Both build important life skills!
